# Software Tools Engineer (Firmware)
...
If you’re looking for a role where your code doesn’t just sit in a Git repo—this is it. Our team builds internal tools that power the development of world-class hard disk drives. You won’t be designing the hardware itself—but you will be designing the software tools that help engineers make smarter, faster, data-driven decisions that shape the future of storage technology.
...
In this role, you will design and develop software applications and data collection frameworks that analyze servo‑mechanical behavior and system performance in eHDDs. These applications run in Windows and Linux environments and scale from individual machines to large, multi‑device test setups. The tools you build help engineering teams evaluate designs, validate software and firmware changes, and accelerate development cycles through automation and visibility.

From enabling systems to make cities safer and more connected, to powering the data centers behind many of the world’s biggest companies and hyperscale cloud providers, to meeting the massive and ever-growing data storage needs of the AI era, WD is fueling a brighter, smarter future.

- We are seeking a Principal Architect, Intelligent Platforms to help shape the technical architecture for a next generation platform strategy that unlocks the full value of intelligent storage infrastructure. This leader will support the development of a platform architecture that begins with HDD, extends to flash, and evolves across future storage tiers through a consistent, scalable, and developer friendly API foundation.
...
- The ideal candidate brings deep systems architecture expertise across storage, distributed systems, software platforms, and APIs. This person will work across product, engineering, research, and business teams to help translate strategic goals into a durable technical architecture, clear design principles, and an executable roadmap.
...
- Support the definition and evolution of the architecture for Intelligent Platforms across device, software, and network interface layers.
...
- Partner with product management, engineering, and fellow architects to shape a scalable API and platform architecture that emphasizes consistency, extensibility, and long term value.
...
- Provide architectural guidance and technical leadership on key design decisions, tradeoffs, and integration points across HDD, flash, and future storage tiers.
...
- Contribute to architectural reviews, technical standards, and design principles that help ensure coherence across teams and releases.
...
- Represent the architecture in cross functional discussions with internal stakeholders, partners, and customers, helping connect strategic goals with technical direction.
...

...

- 10+ years of experience in systems architecture, platform software, storage systems, distributed systems, or related infrastructure domains.
- Proven experience contributing to the architecture of large scale platforms, infrastructure software, or storage products.
- Strong background in APIs, software architecture, and system level design for complex products.
- Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence across engineering, product, and leadership teams.

...
- Advanced degree in a relevant technical field.

- Familiarity with hyperscale infrastructure patterns, internal platform APIs, or cloud scale services.
- Experience supporting architectural decisions across both hardware and software domains.
- Familiarity with AI and data intensive workloads and the infrastructure needs that support them.

results.title: Pradhan B.
highlights:
Principal Engineer, Research & Development at Western Digital
...
Principal Engineer, Research and Development at [Western Digital](https://www.linkedin.com/company/western-digital)
...
I am an R&D Engineer with an innovation-driven mindset and over a decade of hands-on industry experience in developing advanced signal processing and machine learning solutions for high-density data storage systems. My expertise spans adaptive equalization, soft-output detection algorithms, neural network-based read channel architectures, and mutual information-driven optimization across magnetic and solid-state media platforms. In my current role, I focus on: Modeling Read/Write channels for ISI (Inter-Symbol Interference) and ITI (Inter-Track Interference) magnetic channels Prototyping and implementing advanced signal processing techniques for single and multi-reader sensor arrays (TDMR – Two-Dimensional Magnetic Recording Systems) Evaluating and assessing the effectiveness of algorithms, including data recovery techniques, pattern-dependent noise reduction, and compensation circuits Benchmarking conventional signal processing methods against neural network and machine learning-based solutions Collaborating with graduate-level university research on next-generation signal processing and ML techniques I earned my Master’s degree from the University of Minnesota – Twin Cities in 2015, specializing in Communication Systems and Signal Processing. During my graduate studies, I completed an internship at Western Digital (formerly HGST, a Western Digital Company) as part of the Read/Write Channel Architecture Team, where I contributed to next-generation data channel technologies. My responsibilities included evaluating and benchmarking channel performance by implementing and testing advanced signal processing solutions, comparing them against prevailing industry standards.

results.title: AI Storage Demand Outlasts the Data Center Build-Out | WD
highlights:
# AI Doesn’t End at Compute—It Compounds as Data
...
1. AI
...
demand is cumulative and persistent, unlike compute demand, which is event-driven and tied to
...
new data—output
...
and logs—all
...
which require long
...
3. WD
...
significant growth in
...
demand, driven significantly
...
To answer that question, it’s important to understand the role of HDD storage in AI. And that story starts with data. Data is both the foundation of AI—and its output. All of the GPUs and memory and networking are useless without the massive data lakes and training sets that inform the models. And without demand for the output of the models—even more data—the models wouldn’t exist. So, AI not only relies on data to function, it’s also generating new data with every input prompt. Every training run, every inference, every interaction creates new data that must be stored, managed, and retained. In the cloud, that data overwhelmingly lives on HDDs.4
...
can think of an operational AI model the same way we might think of a car. A car is a collection of components (engine, transmission, seats, etc.) and a computer controlling it all. Once a customer buys the car, all they need to do is keep it fueled and maintained, and it can fulfill its purpose of transportation for many years and miles. In many ways, an operational AI model is the same in that it requires a collection of components, GPU/DRAM/flash/networking, and a trained LLM
...
it all.
...
you have bought the hardware and model, you have the ability to generate responses to user prompts for many years. Buying the car or the model is an event—but it then becomes an asset that may deliver benefits for many years.
...
When we look at the relationship between AI data center build-out and data storage demand, we must remember why we call these data centers “AI factories” in the first place. When we think of factories, we think of large buildings that exist to produce something else. In this case, that “something else” is data. Which means while existing automotive factories could be described as car generators, existing AI data centers are data generators. Thus, every already-existing AI data center is now filling up hard drives with newly stored data. The
...
isn’t the
...
; the output—data—is the story.
...
We can even think of every existing AI data center as a circular generator of data. They are trained on large-scale datasets that need to be persistently stored, because future versions of models will need to be trained. During training and inference, they generate a tremendous amount of metadata that must be retained to understand model behavior and drive further improvement. And during inference, the model is generating new data that must be stored. Both the metadata and the newly generated data—as well as synthetic data expressly generated for the purpose of model training—can then be incorporated to improve and update the training of future models.
...
behaves differently. It is persistent. It accumulates
...
Meanwhile, the demand for storage—HDD storage in particular—grows during the course of operating the AI factory. It is an ongoing and cumulative demand, driven by inference. The demand for HDDs is based on how much the AI data centers are being used.
...
upgrades.6
...
The tailwind of AI data center build-out is real, and it is affecting the rate of growth of the HDD storage industry. But it’s important to understand that HDD storage was already a secular growth story, and is already experiencing stronger demand based upon existing AI data center deployments continuously generating new data. Even if AI infrastructure build-out slows, the data already created—and continuously generated—must still be stored, accessed, and retained. This makes storage demand structurally different from compute demand.

highlights:
Artificial Intelligence - Products &
...
# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
...
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
...
AI Strategy & Vision: Partner with the CEO, CTO, CIO, and business unit heads to define IDC's multi-year AI roadmap—spanning generative AI, agentic systems, predictive analytics, and AI-native product development. Technical Leadership: Architect and oversee IDC's AI platform and infrastructure, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with enterprise data strategy. Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring
...

7+ years of experience in applied machine learning, AI engineering, or data science—with at least 3 years in senior technical leadership roles.
